description: Task Priority System - first evolving PRD slice

finalSuccessCriteria.description: Users can assign, view, edit, and filter task priority across the app, with priority persisted between sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Tasks persist a priority value of high, medium, or low
Users can see priority on task cards without opening details
Users can edit priority from the task edit flow
Users can filter the task list by priority
Relevant UI flows are verified in browser
finalSuccessCriteria.notes: All criteria met: storyPriority field persisted in prd.json (US-001); validate prints priority tags (US-002); ralph priority command edits priority (US-003); validate --priority flag filters by priority (US-004). Typecheck passes. CLI end-to-end verified.

planning.currentObjective: Allow users to filter the story list by priority via a CLI flag on validate

prdChain.objective: Persist task priority in the data model
prdChain.notes: US-001 passed: storyPriority enum field added to schema and validated.

prdChain.objective: Show priority label when the CLI lists user stories
prdChain.notes: US-002 passed: validate command prints [HIGH]/[MEDIUM]/[LOW] beside each story.

prdChain.objective: Allow editing storyPriority of a user story via CLI
prdChain.notes: US-003 passed: ralph priority <storyId> <high|medium|low> command implemented.

prdChain.objective: Filter story list by priority in the validate output
prdChain.notes: US-004 passed: ralph validate --priority <level> filters story list.

userStories.title: Filter story list by priority
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pass a --priority flag to `ralph validate` to show only stories matching that priority level, so I can focus on high-priority work.
acceptanceCriteria: `ralph validate --priority high` lists only stories with storyPriority=high
`ralph validate --priority medium` lists only stories with storyPriority=medium
`ralph validate --priority low` lists only stories with storyPriority=low
When no stories match the filter, a message is printed indicating zero results
Without --priority flag, all stories are listed (existing behavior preserved)
Typecheck passes (`npm run typecheck` exits 0 in cli/)
End-to-end CLI output verified: running validate with and without filter shows correct results
userStories.notes: validate --priority flag implemented; zero-match message shown; typecheck and build pass.
